violations.description: Dispensing equipment - Design and construction. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not properly designed and/or constructed to prevent contamination. - Cover for ice above soda machine is missing the front piece. Needs to be fixed to prevent contamination to the ice inside the machine.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration. - 3-Comp Sink tested at 0 ppm QA. Sanitizer jug empty under sink. New jug needs to be installed.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. - Hand sink in kitchen was blocked by a floor cleaning machine. Handwashing sinks need to be accessible for employees.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. - No papertowels despenced from machine above handwashing sink. Out of papertowels or machine not working correctly.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. - Ceiling in kitchen area has the popcorn texture. Needs to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. - Mop was not hung in back room to allow air drying.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. - Boxes of food on floor in walk-in freezer.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. - Containers in prep coolers need to be labeled and dated.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. - Several food items in walk in cooler need to be discarded. Cheese and sour cream are past the sell by date.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. - Vent filters in need of cleaning.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. - Box of paper towels on floor near hot-hold unit for pizza. - Box of cups on floor in back room.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. - Hand sink in kitchen was blocked by a floor cleaning machine. Handwashing sinks need to be accessible for employees.--Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. - Sand/dirt debris found in handwashing sink. Handwashing sinks cannot be used for anything other than washing hands.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. - Ceiling in kitchen area has the popcorn texture. Needs to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. - Mop was not hung in back room to allow air drying.
